title: Dividing by 10, 100, and 1000 — The Pattern of Removing Zeros

steps.work: Remove one zero from the end: 460 → 46.
steps.explanation: Dividing by 10 shifts every digit one place to the right. The ones digit (0) drops off.
Dividing by 100 shifts digits two places right. Two zeros drop off.
Dividing by 1000 shifts digits three places right. Three zeros drop off.

steps.work: ÷ 10 → remove 1 zero. ÷ 100 → remove 2 zeros. ÷ 1000 → remove 3 zeros.
steps.explanation: The number of zeros in the divisor tells you how many zeros to remove (or how many places the digits shift right).

title: Applying Place-Value Division to a Word Problem

steps.instruction: A warehouse has 15000 water bottles. They ship them in crates of 1000. How many crates?
steps.explanation: The divisor is 1000, so we will remove three zeros.
15000 has four zeros, but we only remove three (matching the three zeros in 1000).
